CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-07-2020 Continued To Apr-08-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp 128 5 And 128 7
Off Calendar Feb-11-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kelsey Daniel, Esq. 619-525-7626 of Siegel Moreno & Stettler for Intervenor through Court Call.
No Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT EMPIRE ENGINEERING AND CONSTRUCTION INC.'S Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To CCP 128.5 And 128.7.

Cross-defendant Empire Engineering and Construction, Inc.'s motion for sanctions is denied. By order dated January 7, 2020, the court sustained Empire's demurrer to the cross-complaint, but granted cross-complainant Cal-Con Pumping, Inc. leave to amend, and it filed a first amended cross-complaint on January 27, 2020. The validity of that amended pleading has not yet been adjudicated. That Cal-Con filed an initial pleading that failed to allege an adequate factual basis for its cross-claims does not, by itself, establish a basis for an award of sanctions under either of the statutes Empire invokes. "Let us merely say that we heartily disapprove of knee-jerk requests for sanctions brought by any litigant, and leave it at that." (In re Marriage of Koester (1999) 73 Cal.App.4th 1032, 1041.)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-23-2019 Continued To Feb-26-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-07-2020 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Kelsey Daniel, Esq. of SIEGEL, MORENO & STETTLER, T:619.525.7626, for Intervenor, on Court Call;
No other appearance.

RULING: There being no objection, the tentative ruling on CROSS DEFENDANT EMPIRE ENGINEERING AND CONSTRUCTION INC.'s DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is adopted as follows:

Cross-Defendant Empire Engineering & Construction, Inc.'s demurrer to Cal-Con Pumping, Inc.'s unverified cross-complaint is sustained in its entirety with leave to amend. The cross-complaint fails to allege any factual basis for Cal-Con's claims for indemnity, declaratory relief, or apportionment of fault. The exhibits attached to the declaration of counsel accompanying Cal-Con's untimely opposition to the demurrer are not properly considered by the Court on demurrer. Cal-Con is granted 20 days leave to amend, if it can do so in good faith, to allege a basis for its cross-claims.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
